description_long: This object is a stylized mushroom with a large, rounded cap that is predominantly red-orange and adorned with several large and small white spots. The cap has a slightly irregular, naturalistic edge with a subtle white border, giving it a soft, organic appearance. The stem is short, thick, and dark brown, tapering slightly toward the base and appearing sturdy in proportion to the cap. The surface of the cap appears matte and somewhat velvety, lacking any visible gloss or shine. No gills are visible beneath the cap, as the underside is obscured in all views. The overall form and coloration are reminiscent of the iconic Amanita muscaria, often found in parks or naturalized urban green spaces.
description: A red-orange mushroom with white spots and a thick, dark brown stem, featuring a soft, matte cap and a naturalistic edge.
description_view_0: The mushroom is seen from a slightly elevated front angle, showing the cap's red-orange color with prominent white spots and a thick, dark brown stem.
description_view_1: This view highlights the left side of the cap, revealing more of the white spots and the subtle white border along the cap's edge.
description_view_2: The mushroom is viewed from the back, displaying the cap's curvature and the distribution of white spots, with the stem partially visible.
description_view_3: The right side of the mushroom is visible, showing the cap's rounded shape, white spots, and the thick stem beneath.
attribute_cap_pattern: spotted with large and small white dots
